segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ أَنْتَ رَجَائِي فِي كُلِّ كُرْبَةٍ
segments.transliteration: allahumma anta raja'i fi kulli kurbatin

translation.en: You are my hope in all hard times,

segments.transliteration: wa anta thiqati fi kulli shiddatin

translation.en: in You alone I put my trust in all ordeals,

segments.arabic: وَأَنْتَ لِي فِي كُلِّ أَمْرٍ نَزَلَ بِي ثِقَةٌ وَعُدَّةٌ
segments.transliteration: wa anta li fi kulli amrin nazala bi thiqatun wa `uddatun

translation.en: and You alone are my reliance and support in every matter that afflicts me.

segments.arabic: فَكَمْ مِنْ كَرْبٍ يَضْعُفُ عَنْهُ ٱلْفُؤَادُ

translation.en: Too many are the agonies, which the heart is too weak to undergo,
which all stratagems fail to manage,
at which the enemy gloat,
and in which I lose any way out,
but when I referred them to You and complained about them to You,
abandoning all others than You,
You have relieved and saved me from them.
So, You are the patron of all graces,
the One Who stands for all requests,
and the utmost of all desires.
So, very much praise be to You
and abundant gratitude be to You.

text.en: Twenty-ninth: Through a considerable chain of authority, Imam Ja`far al-Sadiq (`a) is reported to have taught this supplicatory prayer to Abu-Basir to say it in his supplications::

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ قَوْلَ ٱلتَّوَّابِينَ وَعَمَلَهُمْ
segments.transliteration: allahumma inni as'aluka qawla alttawwabina wa `amalahum

translation.en: O Allah, I beseech You for the words of those who turn constantly to You and for their deeds,
the light of the prophets and their honesty,
the salvation of the strivers and their rewards,
the gratitude of the chosen ones and their advice,
the deed of the rememberers and their certitude,
the faith of the scholars and their knowledgeability,
the worship of the dutiful ones and their modesty,
the wisdom of the well-versed scholars and their way,
the fear of the pious ones and their desires,
the belief of the believers and their trust (in You),
and the hope of the good-doers and their devoutness.
O Allah, I beseech You for the reward of the thankful ones,
the rank of the ones drawn near to You,
and the companionship of the prophets.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ خَوْفَ ٱلْعَامِلِينَ لَكَ
segments.transliteration: allahumma inni as'aluka khawfa al`amilina laka

translation.en: O Allah, I beseech You for the fear of those who act for Your sake constantly,
the deed of those who fear You,
the submission of those who worship You,
the certitude of those who put their trusts in You,
and the reliance on You of those who believe in You.

segments.arabic: اَللَّهُمَّ إِنَّكَ بِحَاجَتِي عَالِمٌ غَيْرُ مُعَلَّمٍ

translation.en: O Allah, about my request, You know it well and You do not need to be informed about it

segments.transliteration: wa anta laha wasi`un ghayru mutakallafin

translation.en: and You are fully competent to grant it and it does not require You to exert any effort.

segments.transliteration: wa anta alladhi la yuhfika sa'ilun

translation.en: It is You Whom is never disturbed by any suppliant;
and Whose kingdom is never decreased owing to donations;

segments.transliteration: wa la yablughu midhataka qawlu qa'ilin

translation.en: and You are too praised to be expressed by any statement.
You are as exactly as You describe Yourself
but more elevated than what we say about You.
O Allah, (please) grant me close relief,
great reward,
and nice covering.
O Allah, You do know

segments.arabic: أَنِّي عَلَىٰ ظُلْمِي لِنَفْسِي وَإِسْرَافِي عَلَيْهَا

translation.en: that although I have wronged myself and violated it excessively,

segments.arabic: لَمْ أَتَّخِذْ لَكَ ضِدّاً وَلاَ نِدّاً
segments.transliteration: lam attakhidh laka diddan wa la niddan

translation.en: I have never claimed that You have a contrary, a peer,

segments.transliteration: wa la sahibatan wa la waladan

translation.en: a wife, or a son.
O He Who is never confused by requests!

segments.arabic: وَيَا مَنْ لاَ يَشْغَلُهُ شَيْءٌ عَنْ شَيْءٍ
segments.transliteration: wa ya man la yashghaluhu shay'un `an shay'in

translation.en: O He Who is never preoccupied by a thing against another thing,
a hearing against another hearing,
or a sight against another sight!
O Who is never annoyed by the insistence of the earnest petitioners,

segments.arabic: أَسْأَلُكَ أَنْ تُفَرِّجَ عَنِّي فِي سَاعَتِي هٰذِهِ
segments.transliteration: as'aluka an tufarrija `anni fi sa`ati hadhihi

translation.en: I beseech You to grant me relief at this very moment

segments.arabic: مِنْ حَيْثُ أَحْتَسِبُ وَمِنْ حَيْثُ لاَ أَحْتَسِبُ
segments.transliteration: min haythu ahtasibu wa min haythu la ahtasibu

translation.en: from whence I expect and from whence I do not.
Verily, You give life to bones when they have rotted away.
You verily have power over all things.

segments.arabic: يَا مَنْ قَلَّ شُكْرِي لَهُ فَلَمْ يَحْرِمْنِي
segments.transliteration: ya man qalla shukri lahu falam yahrimni

translation.en: O He to Whom I rarely turned thankful; yet, He did not deprive me,
against Whom I committed grand sins; yet, He did not disclose me,
Who saw me insisting on disobeying Him; yet, He did not encountered me,
and Who created me for the purpose He has created me for,
but I committed things purposed for other than the aim for which He has created me.
You are the most excellent Lord, my Master,
but You have found me the most miserable servant.
Most excellent intender You are, my Lord,
but You have found me the most miserable intended one.
I, Your servant and the son of Your two servants,
am before You;
You can do to me whatever You want.
O Allah, all sounds have calmed down,
all motions have been standstill,
and every lover has withdrawn with his beloved;

segments.transliteration: wa khalawtu bika anta almahbubu ilayya

translation.en: so, I am withdrawing with You, my most beloved;

segments.arabic: فَٱجْعَلْ خَلْوَتِي مِنْكَ ٱللَّيْلَةَ ٱلْعِتْقَ مِنَ ٱلنَّارِ
segments.transliteration: faj`al khalwati minka allaylata al`itqa min alnnari

translation.en: therefore, make the reward of my withdrawing with You this night to be release from Hellfire.

segments.arabic: يَا مَنْ لَيْسَتْ لِعَالِمٍ فَوْقَهُ صِفَةٌ
segments.transliteration: ya man laysat li`alimin fawqahu sifatun

translation.en: O He above Whose description, there is no knowledgeable one may enjoy a description!

segments.arabic: يَا مَنْ لَيْسَ لِمَخْلُوقٍ دُونَهُ مَنْعَةٌ
segments.transliteration: ya man laysa limakhluqin dunahu man`atun

translation.en: O He against Whom, there is no creature to prevent itself!
O Foremost before all things!

segments.transliteration: wa ya akhiran ba`da kulli shay'in

translation.en: O Everlasting after all things!
O He Who has no starting point!

segments.transliteration: wa ya man laysa li'akhirihi fana'un

translation.en: O He Who has no final point!
O most perfect of all described things!
O most liberal of all donors!

segments.arabic: وَيَا مَنْ يَفْقَهُ بِكُلِّ لُغَةٍ يُدْعَىٰ بِهَا
segments.transliteration: wa ya man yafqahu bikulli lughatin yud`a biha

translation.en: O He Who understands all languages used for praying Him!
O He Whose pardon is eternal,
Whose grip is strong,
and Whose kingdom is straight!

segments.arabic: أَسْأَلُكَ بِٱسْمِكَ ٱلَّذِي شَافَهْتَ بِهِ مُوسَىٰ
segments.transliteration: as'aluka bismika alladhi shafahta bihi musa

translation.en: I beseech You in the name of Your Name that You have used in Your speaking with Moses.

segments.arabic: يَا اللَّهُ يَا رَحْمٰنُ يَا رَحِيمُ
segments.transliteration: ya allahu ya rahmanu ya rahimu

translation.en: O Allah! O All-beneficent! O All-merciful!
O He save Whom there is no god!
O Allah, You are verily the eternally besought of all.

segments.arabic: أَسْأَلُكَ أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ
segments.transliteration: as'aluka an tusalliya `ala muhammadin wa ali muhammadin

translation.en: I beseech You to bless Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad
and to allow me to Paradise out of Your mercy.

text.en: Thirtieth: Yunus is reported to have asked Imam al-Rida (`a) for a brief supplicatory prayer; so, the Imam (`a) taught him to say this:

translation.en: O He Who has guided me to Himself
and humiliated my heart by testifying to Him,
I beseech You for security and faith.

text.en: 1.Prayer for Settling the Needs

In al-Rawandi’s book of al-Da`awat, it is reported that Imam Zayn al-`ªbidin (`a) passed by a man sitting at the doorstep of another man’s house. “What for are you sitting at the door of this lavish tyrant?” the Imam (`a) asked.“It is because of ordeal,” the man answered. “Stand up, “ the Imam (`a) said, “I will guide you to a door better than this tyrant’s door and to a Lord who is better than this man.”

Hence, the Imam (`a) took the man from the hand to the Holy Prophet’s Mosque where he instructed him to turn his face towards the kiblah direction, offer a two-unit prayer, raise the hands towards Almighty Allah to praise Him, invoke His blessings upon the Holy Prophet and his Household, pray to Him by reciting the last verses of Surah al-Hashr (No. 59), the first six verses of Surah al-Hadid (No. 57), and the two verses in Surah ªl-`Imran (either verses 81-19 according to al-Rawandi, or 26-27 according to al-Majlisi), and then pray to Him for the personal needs. Any prayer that is said in this situation will be definitely granted.

It is worth mentioning that Imam `Ali Amir al-Mu'minin (`a) said, “If you need something to be settled, you may go for seeking it on early Thursdays and you may recite the last verses of Surah ªAle Imran, Ayah al-Kursi, Surah al-Qadr, and Surah al-Fatihah before you leave your house, because these holy verses and Surahs contribute to solving all the needs for this life and the life to come.”

You may offer a four-unit prayer with precise Qunut and other pillars (i.e. genuflection, prostration, and the other parts of prayers). In the first unit, you may recite Surah al-Fatihah one time and repeat the following verse seven times:
In the second unit, you may recite Surah al-Fatihah once and the following verse seven times:

segments.arabic: مَا شَاءَ ٱللَّهُ لاَ قُوَّةَ إِلاَّ بِٱللَّهِ إِنْ تَرَنِ أَنَٱ أَقَلَّ مِنْكَ مَالاً وَوَلَداً.
segments.transliteration: ma sha'a allahu la quwwata illa billahi in tarani ana aqalla minka malan wa waladan

text.en: In the third unit, you may recite Surah al-Fatihah once and the following verse seven times:

segments.arabic: لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ سُبْحَانَكَ إِنِّي كُنْتُ مِنَ ٱلظَّالِمِينَ.
segments.transliteration: la ilaha illa anta subhanaka inni kuntu min alzzalimina

text.en: In the fourth unit, you may recite Surah al-Fatihah once and the following verse seven times:

segments.arabic: وَأُفَوِّضُ أَمْرِي إِلَىٰ اللَّهِ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ بَصِيرٌ بِٱلْعِبَادِ.
segments.transliteration: wa ufawwidu amri ila allahi inna allaha basirun bil`ibadi

translation.en: You may then mention your need.

text.en: Imam al-Sadiq (`a) is reported to have said: When you face a difficulty, you may offer a two-unit prayer at midday. In the first unit, you may recite Surah al-Fatihah, Surah al-Tawhid, and the first three verses of Surah al-Fath (No. 48):

translation.en: Surely, We have given to you a clear victor,

segments.arabic: لِيَغْفِرَ لَكَ ٱللَّهُ مَا تَقَدَّمَ مِنْ ذَنْبِكَ وَمَا تَأَخَّرَ
segments.transliteration: liyaghfira laka allahu ma taqaddama min dhanbika wa ma ta'akhkhara

translation.en: that Allah may forgive your community their past faults and those to follow
and complete His favor to you
and keep you on a right way,
and that Allah might help you with a mighty help.

text.en: In the second unit, you may recite Surah al-Fatihah, Surah al-Tawhid, and <ref=1:94>Surah Al-Sharh</ref> . This prayer has been personally experienced.
The following is quoted from the book of Makarim al-Akhlaq:

At midnight, you may bathe yourself and offer a two-unit prayer. In both units, you should recite Surah al-Fatihah once and repeat Surah al-Tawhid five hundred times. After repeating this Surah in the second unit, you may recite <ref=1:59:21-24>last verses of Surah al-Hashr</ref>
You may then recite the first six verses of Surah al-Hadid. While standing up, you may then repeat the following holy verse one thousand times:

translation.en: You do we serve and You do we beseech for help.

text.en: You may then accomplish your prayer and praise Almighty Allah. If your need is not settled, you may repeat this prayer. If it is still not settled, you may repeat it once more and your need will be settled, Allah willing.

The trustworthy Shaykh al-Kulayni, may Allah have mercy upon him, reports in his book of al-Kafi that `Abd al-Rahim al-Qasir said: I once visited Imam al-Sadiq (`a) and said, “May Allah accept me as ransom for you! I have invented a formula of supplicatory prayer.”

The Imam (`a) answered, “Save me from your inventions! Whenever a disaster befalls you, you should have recourse to the Messenger of Allah and offer a two-unit prayer and dedicate it to him, peace be upon him and his Household.” “How can I do that?” I asked.The Imam (`a) answered, “You should bathe yourself and offer a two-unit prayer, beginning it with the prefatory part of obligatory prayers and say the tashahhud statement of obligatory prayers. When you finish saying the tashahhud and taslim statements, you should say the following:
O Allah, You are the (source of) peace;

translation.en: from You does peace come;
and to You does peace return.

segments.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ
segments.transliteration: allahumma salli `ala muhammadin wa ali muhammadin

translation.en: O Allah, (please) send blessings upon Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad,

segments.transliteration: wa balligh ruha muhammadin minni alssalama

translation.en: convey my greetings to the soul of Muhammad
and the souls of the veracious Imams,
and convey to me their response of my greetings.

segments.transliteration: walssalamu `alayhim wa rahmatu allah wa barakatuhu

translation.en: Peace and Allah’s mercy and blessings be upon them.
O Allah, these two units of prayer
are present from me to Allah’s Messenger,
peace and greetings of Allah be upon him and his Household.
S0, (please) decide Your reward to be that You grant me what I have hoped

segments.transliteration: wa rajawtu fika wa fi rasulika

translation.en: and expected from You and Your Messenger.
O Guardian of the believers.

text.en: You may then prostrate and repeat the following forty times:

translation.en: O Ever-living! O Self-Subsisting!
O Ever-living One Who never dies!

segments.arabic: يَا حَيّاً لاَ إِلٰهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ
segments.transliteration: ya hayyan la ilaha illa anta

translation.en: O Ever-living One; there is no god save You!
O Lord of majesty and honoring!
O most Merciful of all those who show mercy.

text.en: You may then put your right cheek on the ground and repeat the aforesaid supplication forty times. You may then put your left cheek on the ground and repeat the supplication more forty times.You may then raise your head from prostration, extend your hands (towards the sky) and repeat the supplication forty times. You may then pull your hands to your neck, point out with your forefinger, and repeat the supplication forty times. You may then hold your beard with your left hand and weep, or try to weep, and say the following:

translation.en: O Muhammad! O Allah’s Messenger!

segments.transliteration: ashku ila allah wa ilayka hajati

translation.en: I complain to Allah and to you about my need

segments.transliteration: wa ila ahli baytika alrrashidina hajati

translation.en: and I complain to your Household, the rightly guided, about my need,

segments.arabic: وَبِكُمْ أَتَوَجَّهُ إِلَىٰ ٱللَّهِ فِي حَاجَتِي
segments.transliteration: wa bikum atawajjahu ila allahi fi hajati

translation.en: and through you all I turn my face towards Allah to grant me my need.

text.en: You should then prostrate and repeat the following word as much as one breath can take:

translation.en: O Allah! O Allah!

text.en: You may then say the following:

translation.en: Please, send blessings upon Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad, and...

text.en: You may now mention your need. If you do so, Imam al-Sadiq (`a) added, I guarantee that Allah the Almighty and Majestic will settle your need before you leave your place.”
6.In al-Balad al-Amin, al-Kaf`ami states: For urgent needs, the following words may be written on a sheet of paper and then thrown in (current) water:

translation.en: In the Name of Allah; the All-beneficent, the All-merciful.
From the submissive servant
to the All-majestic Lord.
O Lord: Harm has afflicted me,
and You are the most Merciful of the merciful.
In the name of Muhammad and his Household,
(please) bless Muhammad and his Household,
take off my distress and relieve me from my grief,
out of Your mercy, O most Merciful of all those who show mercy.

text.en: Sayyid ibn Tawus, in his book of al-Mazar, Chapter: Rites of al-Kufah Mosque, after mentioning the recommended acts at Imam `Ali’s Niche (mihrab), has mentioned a special prayer for settling the needs to be offered there. This prayer consists of four units, each two alone; i.e. the taslim statement is uttered after each two units.In the first unit, Surah al-Fatihah is recited once and Surah al-Tawhid is repeated ten times. In the second unit, Surah al-Fatihah is recited once and Surah al-Tawhid is repeated twenty-one times. In the third unit, Surah al-Fatihah is recited once and Surah al-Tawhid is repeated thirty-one times. In the fourth unit, Surah al-Fatihah is recited once and Surah al-Tawhid is repeated forty-one times. Upon uttering the taslim statement and saying the glorification statements, you may do the following:(1) Recite Surah al-Tawhid fifty-one times,(2) utter the imploration for forgiveness (i.e. istighfar) fifty times:

translation.en: I pray Allah for forgiveness.

text.en: (3) invoke blessings upon the Holy Prophet and his Household (i.e. Salawat) fifty times:

segments.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ
segments.transliteration: allahumma salli `ala muhammadin wa ali muhammadin

translation.en: O Allah, (please) send blessings upon Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad,

text.en: (4) repeat the following statement fifty times:

segments.arabic: لاَ حَوْلَ وَلاَ قُوَّةَ إِلاَّ بِٱللَّهِ ٱلعَلِيِّ ٱلعَظِيمِ
segments.transliteration: la hawla wa la quwwata illa billah al`aliyyi al`azimi

translation.en: There is neither might nor power except with Allah.

text.en: and (5) say the following supplication:

translation.en: O Allah Who stops His power (to punish) from His creatures,
Who possesses His authority through it,

segments.arabic: وَٱلْمُتَسَلِّطُ بِمَا فِي يَدَيْهِ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ مَوْجُودٍ
segments.transliteration: walmutasallitu bima fi yadayhi `ala kulli mawjudin

translation.en: and Who has full power over all existent beings through what He possesses!
While all others disappoint those who put hope in them,
he who puts hope in You is pleased and never disappointed.
I beseech You in the name of all items that please You,
all things in which You are present,

segments.arabic: وَبِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ تُحِبُّ أَنْ تُذْكَرَ بِهِ
segments.transliteration: wa bikulli shay'in tuhibbu an tudhkara bihi

translation.en: all things in which You like to be mentioned,
and in Your Name, O Allah,
for there is nothing to be equal to You,

segments.arabic: أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَآلِ مُحَمَّدٍ
segments.transliteration: an tusalliya `ala muhammadin wa ali muhammadin

translation.en: (please) send blessings upon Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad,

segments.transliteration: wa tahfazani wa waladi wa ahli wa mali

translation.en: safeguard me, my sons, my wife, and my possessions,
safeguard me with Your safeguarding,

segments.arabic: وَأَنْ تَقْضِيَ حَاجَتِي فِي… كَذا وَكَذا

translation.en: and settle my need, which is...

text.en: It is reported that one who has a request to be granted by Almighty Allah may offer a four-unit prayer, reciting Surah al-Fatihah and <ref=1:6>Surah Al-An'am</ref> in each unit. Upon accomplishment, one may say the following supplication:

segments.arabic: يَا كَرِيمُ يَا كَرِيمُ يَا كَرِيمُ
segments.transliteration: ya karimu ya karimu ya karimu

translation.en: O All-generous! O All-generous! O All-generous!

segments.arabic: يَا عَظِيمُ يَا عَظِيمُ يَا عَظِيمُ

translation.en: O All-great! O All-great! O All-great!
O Greater than all great ones!
O Hearer of prayers!

segments.arabic: يَا مَنْ لاَ تُغَيِّرُهُ ٱللَّيَالِي وَٱلأَيَّامُ
segments.transliteration: ya man la tughayyiruhu allayali wal-ayyamu

translation.en: O Unaffected by nights and days!
(Please) send blessings upon Muhammad and his Household
and have mercy on my feebleness, neediness,
destitution, and poverty,
because You know them better than I do
and You know my need better than I do.
O He Who had mercy upon Jacob, the old man,

segments.arabic: حِينَ رَدَّ عَلَيْهِ يُوسُفَ قُرَّةَ عَيْنِهِ

translation.en: when He returned Joseph, the delight of his eyes, to him!

segments.arabic: يَا مَنْ رَحِمَ أَيُّوْبَ بَعْدَ طُولِ بَلاَئِهِ
segments.transliteration: ya man rahima ayyuba ba`da tuli bala'ihi

translation.en: O He Who had mercy upon Job after a long time of tribulation!
O He Who had mercy upon Muhammad,
Allah’s peace and blessings be upon him,
Who gave shelter to him against orphanhood,

segments.transliteration: wa nasarahu `ala jababirati qurayshin wa tawaghitiha

translation.en: Who gave him victory against the tyrants and despots of Quraysh
and Who gave him control over them!

segments.arabic: يَا مُغِيثُ يَا مُغِيثُ يَا مُغِيثُ…
segments.transliteration: ya mughithu ya mughithu ya mughithu…

translation.en: O Bringer of aid! O Bringer of aid! O Bringer of aid!…

text.en: One may repeat the last statement frequently and then ask Almighty Allah for one’s need, for Almighty Allah will grant so.

Sayyid Ibn Tawus, may Allah have mercy upon him, has reported the following: At Friday nights and the night before the Eidal-Adha Day (i.e. the tenth of Dhu’l-Hijjah), you may offer a two-unit prayer. In each unit, recite Surah al-Fatihah, but when you reach the following verse, you should repeated it one hundred times:

translation.en: You do we serve and You do we beseech for help.

text.en: You may then recite the next verses of Surah al-Fatihah and then recite Surah al-Tawhid two hundred times. Upon accomplishment of the prayer, you may repeat the following statement seventy times:

segments.arabic: لاَ حَوْلَ وَلاَ قُوَّةَ إِلاَّ بِٱللَّهِ ٱلعَلِيِّ ٱلعَظِيمِ
segments.transliteration: la hawla wa la quwwata illa billah al`aliyyi al`azimi

translation.en: There is neither might nor power except with Allah.

text.en: You may then prostrate and repeat the following word of supplication two hundred times:

translation.en: O my Lord!

text.en: Then, you may pray Almighty Allah for whatever you want, and all your requests will be granted, Allah willing.

This prayer has been reported by a big number of scholars, such as Shaykh al-Mufid, al-Tusi, Sayyid Ibn Tawus, and many others, from Imam al-Sadiq (`a). However, according to the report of Sayyid Ibn Tawus, the way of offering this prayer is as follows:

When you have an urgent need and you wish Almighty Allah to grant it for you, you may observe fasting on three successive days; Wednesday, Thursday, and Friday. On Friday, you may bathe yourself, dress yourself with a new, clean costume, go up to the highest point in your house, and offer a two-unit prayer. Then, you may raise your hands towards the sky and say the following supplication:

translation.en: O Allah, I have resided in Your court
due to my full acquaintance with Your Oneness, Self-independence, and

segments.arabic: وَأَنَّهُ لاَ قَادِراً عَلَىٰ قَضَاءِ حَاجَتِي غَيْرُكَ
segments.transliteration: wa annahu la qadiran `ala qada'i hajati ghayruka

translation.en: that there is none to settle my need save You.
I have known, O my Lord,
that whenever Your graces are poured down on me in hoards,
my need to You becomes more urgent.
I have been inflicted by the distress of…

text.en: You should now mention your problem and then keep on, saying:

translation.en: About relieving me from this distress, You have full knowledge and You need no direction
and You are Limitless and You do not have to think about it.

segments.arabic: فَأَسْأَلُكَ بِٱسْمِكَ ٱلَّذِي وَضَعْتَهُ عَلَىٰ ٱلْجِبَالِ فَنُسِفَتْ
segments.transliteration: fa'as'aluka bismika alladhi wa da`tahu `ala aljibali fanusifat

translation.en: I therefore beseech You in the name of Your Name that You put on the mountains; so, they were carried away from the roots,
You put on the heavens; so, they were rent asunder,
on the stars; so, they were spread,
and on the earth; so, it was made a vast expanse.

segments.arabic: وَأَسْأَلُكَ بِٱلْحَقِّ ٱلَّذِي جَعَلْتَهُ عِنْدَ مُحَمَّدٍ
segments.transliteration: wa as'aluka bilhaqqi alladhi ja`altahu `inda muhammadin

translation.en: I also beseech You in the name of the Right that You deposited with Muhammad,
may Allah bless him and his Household,
and with `Ali, al-Hasan, al-Husayn,
`Ali, Muhammad, Ja`far,
Musa, `Ali, Muhammad,
`Ali, al-Hasan, and the Argument,
peace be upon them,

segments.arabic: أَنْ تُصَلِّيَ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَأَهْلِ بَيْتِهِ
segments.transliteration: an tusalliya `ala muhammadin wa ahli baytihi

translation.en: [I beseech You] t0 send blessings upon Muhammad and his Household,
settle my need for me,
make easy what is difficult in it,
and save me from the intricate part of it.
If you do, then all praise be to You.

segments.transliteration: wa in lam taf`al falaka alhamdu

translation.en: If you do it not, then all praise be to You,
because You are neither biased in Your judgment,
nor accused in Your decree,
nor prejudicing in Your justice.

text.en: You may then put your face on the ground and say the following supplication:

segments.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّ يُونُسَ بْنَ مَتَّىٰ عَبْدَكَ

translation.en: O Allah, Jonah the son of Amittai, Your servant,
prayed to You from the belly of the big fish
and he is Your servant, and You responded to him;
and I am Your servant too; so, (please) respond to me.

text.en: Imam al-Sadiq (`a) said, “Many times, I had many needs to be settled by Almighty Allah. I therefore said this supplication and came back having my need settled for me.”

name: Etiquettes of Praying for Settling the Needs

text.en: In Jamal al-Usbu`, Sayyid Ibn Tawus has mentioned an essay regarding the etiquettes of praying for granting one’s requests, which can be summed up as follows:While trying to beseech the All-knowing Lord to grant you your request, you must be at the most modest manner. When you intend to present yourself before a human king to ask him for granting you a request, you naturally try to attain his pleasure through all possible means and efforts. Likewise, you must exert all possible efforts to attain the pleasure of the Almighty and All-majestic Lord when you want Him to grant you your request. Thus, do not beseech Him in a way less earnest than appealing from human kings; lest, you will fail to win your request because you have been of the scoffers. How is it then reasonable that you care for the pleasure of the All-majestic Lord less than your care for winning the pleasure of human kings who are no more than slaves of the Master of the Worlds? Such being the case, you will be regarded as belittling, scoffing, disdaining, and deriding the grandeur of the All-majestic Lord, and, as a result, you will never win your request no matter how many prayers you would offer and how many days you would fast.Moreover, when you fast on a day or offer a prayer for purpose of winning your request from Almighty God, you must not behave as if you are testing Him; rather, you must have full confidence of His response. As a rule, we, human beings, try others only when we are suspicious about their ability to do what they are tried to do. About those who suspect Him, Almighty Allah says, “…the entertainers of evil thoughts about Allah. On them is the evil turn. (48/6)”Significantly, when you beseech the Lord for granting your request, you must have full confidence in His all-encompassing mercy, His all-inclusive magnanimity, and His fulfillment of all of His promises.When you turn your face towards the most generous of all human beings to ask him for an insignificant thing, you will most certainly be sure that he will grant you that thing after you have asked it from him in the most appropriate way. Similarly, you must be surer that Almighty Allah Who is the All-generous and the most generous of all those who act generously that He will grant you your request, which is, in His sight and as compared to His omnipotence, less insignificant than all insignificant things. Hence, beware that you put confidence in any mortal more than you do with Almighty Allah.It is also advisable that when you offer a prayer and fast on a day for purpose of winning your request from God, you must arrange your worldly requests according to their importance. In fact, the most important of all your requests, no matter how urgent they be, are the requests of the one under whose shade you are living and whose hospitality you are experiencing; namely, the Patron of the Age, Imam al-Mahdi, may Allah’s blessings be upon him.Then come your requests with regard to your religious faith. Only then do come your personal requests involved.To explain, when you intend to beseech Almighty Allah to save you from perdition, you may observe fasting with the intention of praying Him to grant you this very request. In fact, you should have known that winning Almighty Allah’s amnesty, pleasure, acceptance, and approval is more important than being saved from perdition, because you will eventually perish whether you like it or not, but if you miss Almighty Allah’s majesty, pleasure, and acceptance, you will lose this worldly life and the eternal life thus exposing yourself to unbearable and unimaginable misfortunes.Similarly, as I have advised you to give the requests of the Patron of the Age priority to your personal requests, I only meant to inform you that the survival of this world and the survival of its inhabitants are contingent upon the existence of the Patron in this world. Hence, how is it reasonable to give precedence to your personal requests over the requests of the one without whom you do not live a single moment? Let it be known to you that the Patron of the Age is in no need for your implorations for him; rather, if you do what I have just advised you to do, you will then have fulfilled your pledge towards him.Finally, it is highly recommended to commence your supplicatory prayers with invoking Almighty Allah’s blessings upon the Holy Infallibles (`a).

translation.en: My God, offenses have clothed me in the garment of my lowliness,
separation from You has wrapped me in the clothing of my misery!
My dreadful crimes have deadened my heart,

segments.arabic: فَأَحْيِه بِتَوْبَةٍ مِنْكَ يا أَمَلِي وَبُغْيَتِي،
segments.transliteration: faah'-yihee bitaw-batim-minka yaaa amalee wa bugh-yatee

translation.en: so bring it to life by a repentance from You! O my hope and my aim!
O my wish and my want!

segments.arabic: فَوَعِزَّتِكَ ما أَجِدُ لِذُنُوبِي سِواكَ غافِراً،
segments.transliteration: fawai'z-zatik maaa ajidu lid'unoobee siwaaka ghafiraa

translation.en: By Your might, I find no one but You to forgive my sins

segments.transliteration: wa laaa araa likas-ree ghay-raka jaabiraa

translation.en: and I see none but You to mend my brokenness!
I have subjected myself to You in repeated turning,
I have humbled myself to You in abasement.

segments.arabic: فَإنْ طَرَدْتَنِي مِنْ بابِكَ فَبِمَنْ أَلُوذُ؟
segments.transliteration: fa in t'arat-tanee mim-baabika fabiman alood'

translation.en: If You cast me out from Your door, in whom shall I take shelter?

segments.arabic: وَإنْ رَدَدْتَنِي عَنْ جَنابِكَ فَبِمَنْ أَعُوذُ؟
segments.transliteration: wa ir-radat-tanee a'n janaabika fabiman au'wd'

translation.en: If You repell me from Your side, in whom shall I seek refuge?

segments.transliteration: fawaaa asafaahoo min khaj-latee wa af-tiz''aah'ee

translation.en: O my grief at my ignominy and disgrace!

segments.arabic: وَوا لَهْفاهُ مِنْ سُوءِ عَمَلِي وَاجْتِراحِي.
segments.transliteration: wa waa lah-fahoo min sooo-i a'malee wa aj-tiraah'ee

translation.en: O my sorrow at my evil works and what I have committed!
I ask You, O Forgiver of great sins,
O Mender of broken bones,
to overlook my ruinous misdeeds
and cover my disgraceful secret thoughts!

segments.arabic: وَلا تُخْلِنِي فِي مَشْهَدِ الْقِيامَةِ مِنْ بَرْدِ عَفْوِكَ وَغَفْرِكَ،
segments.transliteration: wa laa tukh-linee fee mash-hadil-qeeaamati mim-bar-di a'f-wika wa ghaf-rik

translation.en: At the witnessing place of the Resurrection, empty me not of the coolness of Your pardon and forgiveness,

segments.arabic: وَلا تُعْرِنِي مِنْ جَمِيلِ صَفْحِكَ وَسَتْرِكَ.
segments.transliteration: wa laa tua'-rinee min jameeli s'af-h'ika wa sat-rik

translation.en: and strip me not of Your beautiful forbearance and covering!

segments.arabic: إِلـٰهِي ظَلِّلْ عَلَىٰ ذُنُوبِي غَمامَ رَحْمَتِكَ،
segments.transliteration: ilaahee z'al-lil a'laa d'unoobee ghamaama rah'-matika

translation.en: My God, let the cloud of Your mercy cast its shadow upon my sins

segments.transliteration: wa ar-sil a'laa u'yoobee sah'aaba raa-fatik

translation.en: and send the billow of Your clemency flowing over my faults!

segments.arabic: إِلـٰهِي هَلْ يَرْجِعُ الْعَبْدُ الأَبِقُ إلاَّ إلَىٰ مَوْلاهُ
segments.transliteration: ilaahee hal yar-jiu'l-a'b-dul-aaabiqu il-laaa ilaa maw-laahoo

translation.en: My God, does the fugitive servant return to anyone other than his Master?

segments.arabic: أَمْ هَلْ يُجِيرُهُ مِنْ سَخَطِهِ أَحَدٌ سِواهُ؟
segments.transliteration: am hal yujeeruhoo min sakhat'iheee ah'dun siwaah

translation.en: Or does anyone grant him sanctuary from his Master’s wrath other than his Master Himself?

segments.arabic: إِلـٰهِي إنْ كانَ النَّدَمُ عَلَىٰ الذَّنْب تَوْبَةً،

translation.en: My God, if remorse for sins is a repentance,
I - by Your might - am one of the remorseful!

segments.arabic: وَإِنْ كَانَ الاسْتِغْفارُ مِنَ الْخَطيئَةِ حِطَّةً،

translation.en: If praying forgiveness for offenses is an alleviation,
I am one of those who pray forgiveness!
To You I return that You may be well pleased!
My God, through Your power over me, turn toward me,
through Your clemency toward me, pardon me,
and through Your knowledge of me, be gentle toward me!

segments.arabic: إِلـٰهِي أَنْتَ الَّذِي فَتَحْتَ لِعِبادِكَ بَابَاً إلَىٰ عَفْوِكَ سَمَّيْتَهُ التَّوْبَةَ،
segments.transliteration: ilaaheee antal-lad'ee fatah'-ta lii'baadika baaban ilaa a'f-wika sam-may-tahut-taw-bata:

translation.en: My God, You are He who has opened a door to Your pardon and named it 'repentance',

segments.arabic: فَقُلْتَ: ( تُوبُوا إلَىٰ اللَّهِ تَوْبَةً نَصُوحَاً )،

translation.en: for You said, Repent to God with unswerving repentance.

segments.arabic: فَما عُذْرُ مَنْ أَغْفَلَ دُخُولَ الْبابِ بَعْدَ فَتْحِهِ.
segments.transliteration: famaa u'd'-ru man agh-fala dukhoolal-baabi baa'-da fat-h'ih

translation.en: What is the excuse of him who remains heedless of entering the door after its opening?

segments.arabic: إِلـٰهِي إنْ كانَ قَبُحَ الذَّنْبُ مِنْ عَبْدِكَ
segments.transliteration: ilaaheee in kaana qabuh'ad'-d'ambu min a'b-dika

translation.en: My God, though the sins of Your servant are ugly,
Your pardon is beautiful.

segments.arabic: إِلـٰهِي ما أَنَا بِأَوَّلِ مَنْ عَصاكَ، فَتُبْتَ عَلَيْهِ،
segments.transliteration: ilaahee maaa ana biw-wali man- a's'aaka fatub-ta a'lay-h

translation.en: My God, I am not the first to have disobeyed You, and You turned toward him,
or to have sought to attain Your favour, and You were munificent toward him.
O Responder to the distressed!
O Remover of injury!
O Great in goodness!
O Knower of everything secret!
O Beautiful through covering over!
I seek Your munificence and Your generosity to intercede with You,

segments.transliteration: wa tawas-sal-tu bijanabika wa tarah'-h'umika laday-ka

translation.en: I seek Your side and Your showing mercy to mediate with You,
so grant my supplication,
disappoint not my hope in You,
accept my repentance,
and hide my offense,
through Your kindness and mercy,
O Most Merciful of the merciful

segments.arabic: أَللَّهُمَّ إنَّهُ يَحْجُبُنِي عَنْ مَسْأَلَتِكَ خِلاَلٌ ثَلاثٌ

translation.en: O God, three traits have prevented me from asking Thee
and one trait has urged me on:

segments.arabic: يَحْجُبُنِي أَمْرٌ أَمَرْتَ بِهِ فَأَبْطَأتُ عَنْهُ،

translation.en: I am prevented by a command Thou hast commanded in which I have been slow,
a prohibition Thou hast prohibited toward which I have hurried,

segments.arabic: وَنِعْمَةٌ أَنْعَمْتَ بِهَا عَلَيَّ فَقَصَّرْتُ فِي شُكْرِهَـا.

translation.en: and a favour through which Thou hast favoured for which I have not given sufficient thanks.
I am urged to ask Thee by Thy gratuitous bounty
upon him who turns his face toward Thee
and comes to Thee with a good opinion,
since all Thy beneficence is gratuitous bounty
and every one of Thy favours a new beginning!

segments.arabic: فَهَا أَنَا ذَا يَا إلهِيْ وَاقِفٌ بِبَابِ عِزِّكَ

translation.en: So here I am, my God, standing at the gate of Thy might,

segments.arabic: وُقُوفَ المُسْتَسْلِمِ الذَّلِيْل، وَسَائِلُكَ عَلَى الْحَيَاءِ مِنّي

translation.en: the standing of the lowly, the surrendered, asking Thee in my shame,

segments.arabic: سُؤَالَ الْبَائِسِ الْمُعِيْلِ. مُقـرٌّ لَكَ بأَنّي

translation.en: the asking of the destitute, the pitiful, admitting to Thee that

segments.arabic: لَمْ أَسْتَسْلِمْ وَقْتَ إحْسَانِـكَ إلاَّ بِالاِقْلاَعِ عَنْ عِصْيَانِكَ،

translation.en: at the time of Thy beneficence I surrendered not save through abstaining from disobedience toward Thee

segments.arabic: وَلَمْ أَخْلُ فِي الْحَالاتِ كُلِّهَا مِنِ امْتِنَانِكَ.

translation.en: and in none of my states was I ever without Thy Kindness.

segments.arabic: فَهَلْ يَنْفَعُنِي يَا إلهِي إقْرَارِي عِنْدَكَ بِسُوءِ مَا اكْتَسَبْتُ؟

translation.en: 6- Will it profit me, my God, to admit to Thee the evil of what I have earned?

segments.arabic: وَهَلْ يُنْجِيْنِي مِنْكَ اعْتِرَافِي لَكَ بِقَبِيْحِ مَا ارْتَكَبْتُ؟

translation.en: Will it save me from Thee to confess the ugliness of what I have done?

segments.arabic: أَمْ أَوْجَبْتَ لِي فِي مَقَامِي هَذَا سُخْطَكَ؟

translation.en: Or wilt Thou impose upon me in this my stationThy displeasure?

segments.arabic: أَمْ لَزِمَنِي فِي وَقْتِ دُعَائِي مَقْتُكَ؟

translation.en: Will Thy hate hold fast to me in the time of my supplication?

segments.arabic: سُبْحَانَكَ! لاَ أَيْأَسُ مِنْكَ وَقَدْ فَتَحْتَ لِيَ بَابَ التَّوْبَةِ إلَيْكَ،

translation.en: Glory be to Thee! I do not despair of Thee, for Thou hast opened the door of repentance toward Thyself.

segments.arabic: بَلْ أَقُولُ مَقَالَ الْعَبْدِ الذَّلِيلِ الظَّالِمِ لِنَفْسِهِ

translation.en: Rather, I say, the words of a lowly servant,   having wronged himself
and made light of his Lord's inviolability,

segments.arabic: الَّذِي عَظُمَتْ ذُنُوبُهُ فَجَلَّتْ وَأَدْبَرَتْ أَيّامُهُ فَوَلَّتْ

translation.en: and whose sins are dreadful, great,whose days have parted, fled,

segments.arabic: حَتَّى إذَا رَأى مُدَّةَ الْعَمَلِ قَدِ انْقَضَتْ

translation.en: until, when he sees the term of his works expired and the limit of his lifetime reached
and knows with certainty that he has no escape from Thee,

segments.arabic: وَأَيْقَنَ أَنَّهُ لا مَحيصَ لَهُ مِنْكَ ،وَلاَ مَهْرَبَ لَهُ عَنْكَ

translation.en: no place to flee from Thee, he turns his face toward Thee in repeated turning,
makes his repentance toward Thee sincere,stands before Thee with a pure and purified heart,
then supplicates Thee with a feeble, quiet voice.
He is bowed before Thee, bent,

segments.arabic: قَدْ تَطَأطَأَ لَكَ فَانْحَنى، وَنَكَّسَ رَأسَهُ فَانْثَنَى ،

translation.en: his head lowered, thrown down,

segments.arabic: قَدْ أَرْعَشَتْ خَشْيَتُهُ رِجْلَيْهِ، وَغَرَّقَتْ دُمُوعُهُ خَدَّيْهِ ،

translation.en: his legs shaking in fear,his tears flooding his cheeks.

segments.arabic: يَدْعُوكَ بِيَا أَرْحَمَ الرَّاحِمِينَ وَيَا أَرْحَمَ مَنِ انْتَابَهُ الْمُسْتَرْحِمُونَ،

translation.en: He supplicates Thee: O Most Merciful of the merciful!O Most Merciful of those toward whom seekers of mercy keep on turning

segments.arabic: وَيَا أَعْطَفَ مَنْ أَطَافَ بِهِ الْمُسْتَغْفِرُونَ ،

translation.en: O Tenderest of those around whom run seekers of forgiveness!

segments.arabic: وَيَا مَنْ عَفْوُهُ أكْثَرُ مِنْ نِقْمَتِهِ،

translation.en: O He whose pardon is greater than His vengeance!

segments.arabic: وَيَا مَنْ رِضَاهُ أَوْفَرُ مِنْ سَخَطِهِ،

translation.en: O He whose good pleasure is more abundant than His anger

segments.arabic: وَيَا مَنْ تَحَمَّدَ إلَى خَلْقِهِ بِحُسْنِ التَّجاوُزِ ،

translation.en: O He who seeks His creatures' praise with excellent forbearance!

segments.arabic: وَيَا مَنْ عَوَّدَ عِبادَهُ قَبُولَ الإنَابَةِ ،

translation.en: O He who has accustomed His servants to the acceptance of their repeated turning!
O He who seeks to heal their corruption through repentance!

segments.arabic: وَيَا مَنْ رَضِيَ مِنْ فِعْلِهِمْ بِالْيَسيرِ،

translation.en: O He who is pleased with the easy of their acts!
O He who recompenses with the much their little!

segments.arabic: وَيَا مَنْ ضَمِنَ لَهُمْ إجَابَةَ الدُّعاءِ،

translation.en: O He who has made himself accountable to them to respond to supplication!

segments.arabic: وَيَا مَنْ وَعَدَهُمْ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ بِتَفَضُّلِهِ حُسْنَ الْجَزاءِ،

translation.en: O He who pledged Himself by His gratuitous bounty to give them excellent repayment!

segments.arabic: مَا أَنَا بِأَعْصَى مَنْ عَصَاكَ فَغَفَرْتَ لَهُ،

translation.en: I am not the most disobedient of those who have disobeyed Thee

segments.arabic: وَمَا أَنَا بِأَلْوَمِ مَنِ اعْتَذَرَ إلَيْكَ فَقَبِلْتَ مِنْهُ،

translation.en: and whom Thou hast forgiven, nor am I the most blameworthy to offer excuses which Thou hast accepted,

segments.arabic: وَمَا أَنَا بِأَظْلَمِ مَنْ تَابَ إلَيْكَ فَعُدْتَ عَلَيْهِ ،

translation.en: nor am I the most wrongdoing of those who have repented to Thee, and to whom Thou hast returned

segments.arabic: أَتُوبُ إلَيْكَ فِي مَقَامِي هَذَا تَوْبَةَ نَادِم عَلَى مَا فَرَطَ مِنْهُ

translation.en: I repent to Thee in this my station,  the repentance of one remorseful over what preceded from him

segments.arabic: مُشْفِق مِمَّا اجْتَمَعَ عَلَيْهِ خَالِصِ الْحَيَاءِ مِمَّا وَقَعَ فِيْهِ ،

translation.en: hastily, apprehensive of what has gathered around him, pure in shame for that into which he has fallen,

segments.arabic: عَالِم بِأَنَّ الْعَفْوَ عَنِ الذَّنْبِ الْعَظِيمِ لاَ يَتَعـاظَمُكَ،

translation.en: knowing that pardoning great sins is nothing great for Thee.

segments.arabic: وَأَنَّ التَّجَـاوُزَ عَنِ الإثْمِ الْجَلِيْلِ لا يَسْتَصْعِبُكَ ،

translation.en: overlooking enormous misdeeds is not difficult for thee

segments.arabic: وَأَنَّ احْتِمَالَ الْجنَايَاتِ الْفَـاحِشَةِ لا يَتَكَأَّدُكَ،

translation.en: putting up with indecent crimes does not trouble Thee,

segments.arabic: وَأَنَّ أَحَبَّ عِبَادِكَ إلَيْكَ مَنْ تَرَكَ الاسْتِكْبَارَ عَلَيْكَ،

translation.en: and the most beloved of Thy servants to Thee is he who refrains from arrogance before thee
pulls aside from persistence, and holds fast to praying forgiveness!

segments.arabic: وَأَنَا أَبْرَأُ إلَيْكَ مِنْ أَنْ أَسْتَكْبِرَ،

translation.en: I am clear before Thee of arrogance,
I seek refuge in Thee from persistence,
I pray forgiveness from Thee for shortcomings,

segments.arabic: وَأَسْتَعِينُ بِكَ عَلَى مَا عَجَزْتُ عَنْهُ.

translation.en: I seek help from Thee in incapacity!
O God bless Muhammad and his Household,

segments.arabic: وَهَبْ لِي مَا يَجبُ عَلَيَّ لَكَ ،

translation.en: dispense with what is incumbent upon me toward Thee,
release me from what I merit from Thee,
and grant me sanctuary from what the evildoers fear!

segments.arabic: فَإنَّكَ مَلِيءٌ بِالْعَفْوِ، مَرْجُوٌّ لِلْمَغْفِرَةِ، مَعْرُوفٌ بِالتَّجَاوُزِ ،

translation.en: For Thou art full of pardon, the hoped-for source of forgiveness, well known for Thy forbearance.
My need has no object but Thee,
my sin no forgiver other than Thee - could that be possible?

segments.arabic: وَلاَ أَخَافُ عَلَى نَفْسِي إلاّ إيَّاكَ

translation.en: I have no fear for myself except from Thee;
Thou art worthy of reverential fear, and worthy to forgive!
Bless Muhammad and his Household,
grant my need,answer my request favorably,
forgive my sin and give me security from fear for myself!
Thou art powerful over everything,

segments.arabic: وَذلِكَ عَلَيْكَ يَسِيرٌ آمِينَ رَبَّ الْعَالَمِينَ .

translation.en: and that is easy for Thee.Amen, Lord of the worlds

name: Resignation from sins and request forgiveness

segments.arabic: أللَّهُمَّ يَا مَنْ بِرَحْمَتِهِ يَسْتَغِيثُ الْمُذْنِبُونَ،

translation.en: 1.O God, O He through whose Mercy sinners seek aid!

segments.arabic: وَيَا مَنْ إلَى ذِكْرِ إحْسَانِهِ يَفْزَعُ الْمُضْطَرُّونَ،

translation.en: 2.O He to the remembrance of whose beneficence the distressed flee!
3.O He in fear of whom the offenders weep!
4.O Comfort of every lonely stranger!
5.O Relief of all who are downcast and distressed!
6.O Aid of everyone abandoned and alone!
7.O Support of every needy outcast!

segments.arabic: أَنْتَ الَّذِي وَسِعْتَ كُلَّ شَيْء رَحْمَةً وَعِلْماً،

translation.en: 8.You art He who "embracest everything in mercy and knowledge"! (40:7)

segments.arabic: وَأَنْتَ الَّذِي جَعَلْتَ لِكُلِّ مَخْلُوق فِي نِعَمِكَ سَهْماً،

translation.en: 9.You art He who hast appointed for each creature a share of Your favours!

segments.arabic: وَأَنْتَ الَّذِيْ عَفْوُهُ أَعْلَى مِنْ عِقَابِهِ،

translation.en: 10.You art He whose pardon is higher than His punishment!

segments.arabic: وَأَنْتَ الَّذِي تَسْعَى رَحْمَتُهُ أَمَامَ غَضَبِهِ،

translation.en: 11.You art He whose mercy runs before His wrath!

segments.arabic: وَأَنْتَ الَّذِي عَطَآؤُهُ أكْثَرُ مِنْ مَنْعِهِ،

translation.en: 12.You art He whose bestowal is greater than His withholding!

segments.arabic: وَأَنْتَ الَّذِيْ اتَّسَعَ الْخَلاَئِقُ كُلُّهُمْ فِي رَحمَتِهِ،

translation.en: 13.You art He by whose mercy all creatures are embraced!

segments.arabic: وَأَنْتَ الَّذِي لا يَرْغَبُ فِي جَزَاءِ مَنْ أَعْطَاهُ،

translation.en: 14.You art He who desires no repayment by him upon whom He bestows!

segments.arabic: وَأَنْتَ الَّذِي لا يُفْرِطُ فِي عِقَابِ مَنْ عَصَاهُ.

translation.en: 15.You art He who does not overdo the punishment of him who disobeys You!

segments.arabic: وَأَنَا يَا إلهِي عَبْدُكَ الَّذِي أَمَرْتَهُ بِالدُّعاءِ فَقَالَ:

translation.en: 16.And I, my God, am Your servant whom You commanded to supplicate and who said:

segments.arabic: لَبَّيْكَ وَسَعْدَيْكَ، هَا أَنَا ذَا يَا رَبِّ مَطْرُوحٌ بَيْنَ يَدَيْكَ،

translation.en: 17.I am at Your service and disposal! Here am I, my Lord, thrown down before You.
18.I am he whose back offenses have weighed down!
19.I am he whose lifetime sins have consumed!

segments.arabic: وَأَنَا الَّذِي بِجَهْلِهِ عَصاكَ وَلَمْ تَكُنْ أَهْلاً مِنْهُ لِذَاكَ.

translation.en: 20.I am he who was disobedient in his ignorance, while You didst not deserve that from him!

segments.arabic: هَلْ أَنْتَ يَا إلهِي رَاحِمٌ مَنْ دَعَاكَ فَأُبْلِغَ فِي الدُّعَاءِ

translation.en: 21.Wilt Thou, my God, be merciful toward him who supplicates You, that I should bring my supplication before You?

segments.arabic: أَمْ أَنْتَ غَافِرٌ لِمَنْ بَكَاكَ فَأُسْرِعَ فِي الْبُكَاءِ

translation.en: 22.Wilt You forgive him who weeps to You that I should hurry to weep?

segments.arabic: أَمْ أَنْتَ مُتَجَاوِزٌ عَمَّنْ عَفَّرَ لَكَ وَجْهَهُ تَذَلُّلاً

translation.en: 23.Wilt You show forbearance toward him who puts his face in the dust before You in lowliness?

segments.arabic: أَم أَنْتَ مُغْن مَنْ شَكَا إلَيْكَ فَقْرَهُ تَوَكُّلاً؟

translation.en: 24.Wilt You free from need him who complains to You of his indigent need with confidence?

segments.arabic: إلهِي لاَ تُخيِّبْ مَنْ لا يَجدُ مُعْطِياً غَيْرَكَ،

translation.en: 25.My God, disappoint not him who finds no bestower other than You,

segments.arabic: وَلاَ تَخْذُلْ مَنْ لا يَسْتَغْنِي عَنْكَ بِأَحَدٍ دُونَكَ.

translation.en: 26.and abandon not him who cannot be freed from his need for You through less than You!
27.My God, so bless Muhammad and his Household,

segments.arabic: وَلاَ تُعْرِضْ عَنِّي وَقَدْ أَقْبَلْتُ عَلَيْكَ ،

translation.en: 28.turn not away from me when I have turned my face toward You,
29.deprive me not when 1 have besought You,

segments.arabic: وَلا تَجْبَهْنِي بِالرَّدِّ وَقَدْ انْتَصَبْتُ بَيْنَ يَدَيْكَ.

translation.en: 30.and slap not my brow with rejection when I have stood before You!
31.You art He who has described Himself by mercy,
32.so bless Muhammad and his Household and have mercy upon me!

segments.arabic: وَأَنْتَ الَّذِي سَمَّيْتَ نَفْسَكَ بِالعَفْوِ، فَاعْفُ عَنِّي.

translation.en: 33.You art He who has named Himself by pardon so pardon me!

segments.arabic: قَـدْ تَرَى يَـا إلهِي فَيْضَ دَمْعِي مِنْ خِيفَتِكَ،

translation.en: 34.You hast seen, my God, the flow of my tears in fear of You,
35.the throbbing of my heart in dread of You,
36.and the infirmity of my limbs in awe of You.

segments.arabic: كُلُّ ذَلِكَ حَيآءً مِنِّي لِسُوءِ عَمَلِي،

translation.en: 37.All this from my shame before You because of my evil works!

segments.arabic: وَلِذَاكَ خَمَدَ صَوْتِي عَنِ الْجَارِ إلَيْكَ،

translation.en: 38.So my voice has become silent, no longer crying to You,
39.and my tongue has gone dumb, no longer whispering in prayer.

segments.arabic: يَا إلهِي فَلَكَ الْحَمْدُ، فَكَم مِنْ عَائِبَةٍ سَتَرْتَهَا عَلَيَّ فَلَم تَفْضَحْنِي،

translation.en: 40.My God, so to You belongs praise! How many of my flaws You hast covered over without exposing me!

segments.arabic: وَكَمْ مِنْ ذنْبِ غَطَّيْتَهُ عَلَيَّ فَلَمْ تَشْهَرْنِي،

translation.en: 41.How many of my sins You hast cloaked without making me notorious!

segments.arabic: وَكَمْ مِنْ شَائِبَة أَلْمَمْتُ بِهَا فَلَمْ تَهْتِكْ عَنِّي سِتْرَهَا،

translation.en: 42.How many faults I have committed, yet You didst not tear away from me their covering,
43.collar me with their detested disgrace,

segments.arabic: وَلَمْ تُبْدِ سَوْأَتِهَا لِمَنْ يَلْتَمِسُ مَعَايِبِي مِنْ جِيْرَتِي،

translation.en: 44.or make their dishonour plain to those of my neighbours who search for my defects
45.and to those who envy Your favour toward me!

segments.arabic: ثُمَّ لَمْ يَنْهَنِي ذَلِكَ عَنْ أَنْ جَرَيْتُ إلَى سُوءِ مَا عَهِدْتَ مِنّي

translation.en: 46.But that did not prevent me from passing on to the evil that You knowest from me!

segments.arabic: فَمَنْ أَجْهَلُ مِنِّي يَا إلهِيْ بِرُشْدِهِ

translation.en: 47.So who is more ignorant than I, my God, of his own right conduct?
48.Who is more heedless than I of his own good fortune?

segments.arabic: وَمَنْ أَبْعَدُ مِنِّي مِنِ اسْتِصْلاَحِ نَفْسِهِ

translation.en: 49.Who is further than I from seeking to set himself right?

segments.arabic: حِيْنَ اُنْفِقُ مَا أَجْرَيْتَ عَلَيَّ مِنْ رِزْقِكَ

translation.en: 50.For I spend the provision You deliverest to me
51.in the disobedience You hast prohibited to me!

segments.arabic: وَمَنْ أَبْعَدُ غَوْراً فِي الْبَاطِلِ وَأَشَدُّ إقْدَاماً عَلَى السُّوءِ مِنّي

translation.en: 52.Who sinks more deeply into falsehood and is more intensely audacious in evil than I?

segments.arabic: حِينَ أَقِفُ بَيْنَ دَعْوَتِكَ وَدَعْوَةِ الشَّيْطَانِ،

translation.en: 53.For I hesitate between Your call and the call of Satan

segments.arabic: فَـأتَّبعُ دَعْوَتَهُ عَلَى غَيْرِ عَمىً مِنّي فِيْ مَعْرِفَة بِهِ،

translation.en: 54.and then follow his call without being blind in my knowledge of him

segments.arabic: وَلا نِسْيَان مِنْ حِفْظِي لَهُ وَأَنَا حِينَئِذ مُوقِنٌ

translation.en: 55.or forgetful in my memory of him, while I am certain

segments.arabic: بِأَنَّ مُنْتَهَى دَعْوَتِكَ إلَى الْجَنَّةِ وَمُنْتَهَى دَعْوَتِهِ إلَى النَّارِ

translation.en: 56.that Your call takes to the Garden and his call takes to the Fire!

segments.arabic: سُبْحَانَكَ مَا أَعْجَبَ مَا أَشْهَدُ بِهِ

translation.en: 57.Glory be to You! How marvellous the witness I bear

segments.arabic: عَلَى نَفْسِي وَاُعَدِّدُهُ مِنْ مَكْتُوْمِ أَمْرِي،

translation.en: 58.against my own soul and the enumeration of my own hidden affairs!
59.And more marvellous than that is Your lack of haste with me,
60.Your slowness in attending to me! That is not because I possess

segments.arabic: مِنْ كَرَمِي عَلَيْكَ بَلْ تَأَنِّياً مِنْكَ لِي،

translation.en: 61.honour with You, but because You waitest patiently for me
62.and art bountiful toward me
63.that I may refrain from disobedience displeasing to You
64.and abstain from evil deeds that disgrace me,

segments.arabic: وَلِاَنَّ عَفْوَكَ عَنّي أَحَبُّ إلَيْكَ مِنْ عُقُوبَتِي،

translation.en: 65.and because You lovest to pardon me more than to punish!

segments.arabic: بَلْ أَنَا يَا إلهِي أكْثَرُ ذُنُوباً

translation.en: 66.But I, my God, am more numerous in sins,
67.uglier in footsteps, more repulsive in acts,
68.more reckless in rushing into falsehood,
69.weaker in awakening to Your obedience,
70.and less attentive and heedful toward Your threats,
71.than that I could number for You my faults
72.or have the power to recount my sins.

segments.arabic: وَإنَّمَا أُوبِّخُ بِهَذا نَفْسِي طَمَعَـاً فِي رَأْفَتِكَ

translation.en: 73.I only scold my own soul, craving Your gentleness,
74.through which the affairs of sinners are set right,

segments.arabic: وَرَجَاءً لِرَحْمَتِكَ الَّتِي بِهَا فَكَاكُ رِقَابِ الْخَاطِئِينَ.

translation.en: 75.and hoping for Your mercy, through which the necks of the offenders are freed.

segments.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ وَهَذِهِ رَقَبَتِي قَدْ أَرَقَّتْهَا الذُّنُوبُ،

translation.en: 76.O God, this is my neck, enslaved by sins,

segments.arabic: فَصَلِّ عَلَى مُحَمَّد وَآلِهِ وَأَعْتِقْهَا بِعَفْوِكَ،

translation.en: 77.so bless Muhammad and his Household and release it through Your pardon!
78.This is my back, weighed down by offenses,

segments.arabic: فَصَلِّ عَلَى مُحَمَّد وَآلِهِ وَخَفِّفْ عَنْهُ بِمَنِّكَ.

translation.en: 79.so bless Muhammad and his Household and lighten it through Your kindness!

segments.arabic: يَا إلهِي لَوْ بَكَيْتُ إلَيْكَ حَتَّى تَسْقُطَ أَشْفَارُ عَيْنَيَّ ،

translation.en: 80.My God, were I to weep to You until my eyelids drop off,
81.wail until my voice wears out,
82.stand before You until my feet swell up,
83.bow to You until my backbone is thrown out of joint,
84.prostrate to You until my eyeballs fall out,
85.eat the dirt of the earth for my whole life,
86.drink the water of ashes till the end of my days,

segments.arabic: وَذَكَرْتُكَ فِي خِلاَلِ ذَلِكَ حَتَّى يَكِلَّ لِسَانِي

translation.en: 87.mention You through all of that until my tongue fails,

segments.arabic: ثُمَّ لَمْ أَرْفَعْ طَرْفِي إلَى آفَاقِ السَّمَاءِ اسْتِحْيَاءً مِنْكَ

translation.en: 88.and not lift my glance to the sky's horizons in shame before You,

segments.arabic: مَا اسْتَوْجَبْتُ بِذَلِكَ مَحْوَ سَيِّئَة وَاحِـدَة مِنْ سَيِّئـاتِي،

translation.en: 89.yet would I not merit through all of that the erasing of a single one of my evil deeds!

segments.arabic: وَإنْ كُنْتَ تَغْفِـرُ لِي حِيْنَ أَسْتَوْجِبُ مَغْفِرَتَكَ

translation.en: 90.Though You forgivest me when I merit Your forgiveness
91.and pardonest me when I deserve Your pardon,

segments.arabic: فَإنَّ ذَلِكَ غَيْرُ وَاجِب لِيْ بِاسْتِحْقَاق،

translation.en: 92.yet I have no title to that through what I deserve,
93.nor am I worthy of it through merit,

segments.arabic: إذْ كَـانَ جَزَائِي مِنْكَ فِي أَوَّلِ مَا عَصَيْتُكَ النَّارَ;

translation.en: 94.since my repayment from You from the first that I disobeyed You is the Fire!

segments.arabic: فَإنْ تُعَذِّبْنِي، فَأَنْتَ غَيْرُ ظَالِم لِيْ.

translation.en: 95.So if You punishest me, You dost me no wrong.

segments.arabic: إلهِي فَـإذْ قَـدْ تَغَمَّـدْتَنِي بِسِتْـرِكَ فَلَمْ تَفْضَحْنِي

translation.en: 96.My God, since You hast shielded me with Your covering and not exposed me,
97.waited patiently for me through Your generosity, and not hurried me to punishment,

segments.arabic: و حَلُمْتَ عَنِّي بِتَفَضُّلِكَ فَلَمْ تُغَيِّـرْ نِعْمَتَـكَ عَلَيَّ،

translation.en: 98.and shown me clemency through Your bounty, and not changed Your favour upon me
99.or muddied Your kindly acts toward me,

segments.arabic: فَارْحَمْ طُولَ تَضَرُّعِيْ وَشِـدَّةَ مَسْكَنَتِي وَسُوءَ مَوْقِفِيْ.

translation.en: 100.have mercy on my drawn out pleading, my intense misery, and my evil situation!
101.O God, bless Muhammad and his Household,
102.protect me from acts of disobedience, employ me in obedience,
103.provide me with excellent turning back [to You], purify me through repentance,
104.strengthen me through preservation from sin, set me right through well being,
105.let me taste the sweetness of forgiveness,
106.make me the freedman of Your pardon and the slave released by Your mercy,
107.and write for me a security from Your displeasure!

segments.arabic: وَبَشِّرْنِي بِذلِكَ فِي الْعَاجِلِ دُونَ الآجِلِ بُشْرى أَعْرِفُهَا

translation.en: 108.Give me the good news of that in the immediate, not the deferred - a good news I recognize -
109.and make known to me therein a sign which I may clearly see!

segments.arabic: إنَّ ذلِكَ لاَ يَضيقُ عَلَيْكَ فِي وُسْعِكَ ،

translation.en: 110.That will not constrain You in Your plenty,
111.distress You in Your power,
112.ascend beyond Your lack of haste,

segments.arabic: وَلا يَؤودُك فِي جَزِيلِ هِباتِكَ الَّتِي دَلَّتْ عَلَيْهَا آيَاتُكَ

translation.en: 113.or tire You in Your great gifts, which are pointed to by Your signs.

segments.arabic: إنَّكَ تَفْعَلُ مَا تَشَاءُ وَتَحكُمُ مَا تُرِيدُ،

translation.en: 114.Verily You dost what You wilt, You decreest what You desirest.
115."Thou art powerful over everything". (3:26)

segments.arabic: أَللَّهُمَّ يَا مَنْ لا يَصِفُهُ نَعْتُ الْوَاصِفِينَ ،

translation.en: 1.O God, O He whom the depiction of the describers fails to describe!

segments.arabic: وَيَا مَنْ لاَ يُجَاوِزُهُ رَجَاءُ الرَّاجِينَ ،

translation.en: 2.O He beyond whom passes not the hope of the hopers!

segments.arabic: وَيَا مَنْ لاَ يَضِيعُ لَدَيْهِ أَجْرُ الْمُحْسِنِينَ،

translation.en: 3.O He with whom is not lost the wage of the good-doers!

segments.arabic: وَيَا مَنْ هُوَ مُنْتَهَى خَوْفِ الْعَابِدِيْنَ،

translation.en: 4.O He who is the ultimate object of the fear of the worshipers!

segments.arabic: وَيَا مَنْ هُوَ غَايَةُ خَشْيَةِ الْمُتَّقِينَ.

translation.en: 5.O He who is the utmost limit of the dread of the godfearing!

segments.arabic: هَذا مَقَامُ مَنْ تَدَاوَلَتْهُ أَيْدِي الذُّنُوبِ ،

translation.en: 6.This is the station of him whom sins have passed from hand to hand.
7.Offenses' reins have led him on,
8.and Satan has gained mastery over him.
9.He fell short of what You hast commanded through neglect
10.and he pursued what You hast prohibited in delusion,
11.like one ignorant of Your power over him
12.or one who denies the bounty of Your beneficence toward him,

segments.arabic: حَتَّى إذَا انْفَتَحَ لَهُ بَصَرُ الْهُدَى،

translation.en: 13.until, when the eye of guidance was opened for him

segments.arabic: وَتَقَشَّعَتْ عَنْهُ سَحَائِبُ الْعَمَى أَحْصَى مَا ظَلَمَ بِهِ نَفْسَهُ،

translation.en: 14.and the clouds of blindness were dispelled, he reckoned that through which he had wronged himself
15.and reflected upon that in which he had opposed his Lord.

segments.arabic: فَرَأى كَبِيْرَ عِصْيَانِهِ كَبِيْراً، وَجَلِيل مُخالفَتِهِ جَلِيْلاً،

translation.en: 16.He saw his vast disobedience as vast and his great opposition as great.

segments.arabic: فَأَقْبَلَ نَحْوَكَ مُؤَمِّلاً لَكَ، مُسْتَحْيِيَاً مِنْكَ،

translation.en: 17.So turned to You, hoping in You and ashamed before You,
18.and he directed his beseeching toward You, having trust in You.

segments.arabic: فَأَمَّكَ بِطَمَعِهِ يَقِيناً، وَقَصَدَكَ بِخَوْفِهِ إخْلاَصَاً،

translation.en: 19.He repaired to You in his longing with certitude and he went straight to You in fear with sincerity.

segments.arabic: قَدْ خَلاَ طَمَعُهُ مِنْ كُلِّ مَطْمُوع فِيهِ غَيْرِكَ،

translation.en: 20.His longing was devoid of every object of longing but You,

segments.arabic: وَأَفْرَخَ رَوْعُهُ مِنْ كُلِّ مَحْذُور مِنْهُ سِوَاكَ،

translation.en: 21.and his fright departed from every object of fear but You.
22.So he stood before You pleading,
23.his eyes turned toward the ground in humbleness,
24.his head bowed before Your might in lowliness;

segments.arabic: وَأَبَثَّكَ مِنْ سِرِّهِ مَا أَنْتَ أَعْلَمُ بِهِ مِنْهُ خَضُوعاً،

translation.en: 25.he revealed to You in meekness those secrets of his which You knowest better than he;

segments.arabic: وَعَدَّدَ مِنْ ذُنُوبِهِ مَا أَنْتَ أَحْصَى لَهَا خُشُوعاً

translation.en: 26.he numbered for You in humility those sins of his which You countest better than he;

segments.arabic: وَاسْتَغَاثَ بِكَ مِنْ عَظِيمِ مَاوَقَعَ بِهِ فِي عِلْمِكَ

translation.en: 27.he sought help from You before the dreadful into which he has fallen in Your knowledge
28.and the ugly which has disgraced him in Your judgement:
29.the sins whose pleasures have turned their backs and gone
30.and whose evil consequences have stayed and stuck fast.

segments.arabic: لا يُنْكِرُ يَا إلهِي عَدْلَكَ إنْ عَاقَبْتَهُ،

translation.en: 31.He will not deny Your justice, my God, if You punishest him,

segments.arabic: وَلا يَسْتَعْظِمُ عَفْوَكَ إنْ عَفَوْتَ عَنْهُ وَرَحِمْتَهُ;

translation.en: 32.nor will he consider Your pardon great if You pardonest him and hast mercy upon him,
33.for You art the Generous Lord

segments.arabic: الَّذِي لا يَتَعَاظَمُهُ غُفْرَانُ الذَّنْبِ الْعَظِيم.

translation.en: 34.for whom the forgiveness of great sins is nothing great!

segments.arabic: أَللَّهُمَّ فَهَا أَنَا ذَا قَدْ جئْتُكَ مُطِيعاً

translation.en: 35.O God, so here I am: I have come to You obeying Your command

segments.arabic: لاِمْرِكَ فِيمَا أَمَرْتَ بِهِ مِنَ الدُّعَاءِ،

translation.en: 36.(for You hast commanded supplication)

segments.arabic: مَتَنَجِّزاً وَعْدَكَ فِيمَا وَعَدْتَ بِهِ مِنَ الإجَابَةِ إذْ تَقُولُ

translation.en: 37.and asking the fulfilment of Your promise, (for You hast promised to respond):
38.You hast said, "Supplicate Me and I will respond to you" (40:60).
39.O God, so bless Muhammad and his Household,
40.meet me with Your forgiveness just as I have met You with my confession,

segments.arabic: وَارْفَعْنِي عَنْ مَصَارعِ الذُّنُوبِ كَمَا وَضَعْتُ لَكَ نَفْسِي

translation.en: 41.lift me up from the fatal infirmities of sins just as I have let myself down before You,

segments.arabic: وَاسْتُرْنِي بِسِتْرِكَ كَمَا تَأَنَّيْتَنِي عَنِ الانْتِقَامِ مِنِّي.

translation.en: 42.and cover me with Your covering just as You hast shown no haste to take vengeance on me!
43.O God, make firm my intention to obey You,
44.strengthen my insight in worshipping You,
45.give me the success of works

segments.arabic: لِمَا تَغْسِلُ بِهِ دَنَسَ الخَطَايَا عَنِّي،

translation.en: 46.which will wash away the defilement of offenses,
47.and take me when You takest me in the creed of Your prophet
48.Muhammad (upon him be peace).

segments.arabic: أللَّهُمَّ إنِّي أَتُوبُ إلَيْـكَ فِي مَقَامِي هَذَا

translation.en: 49.O God, I repent to You in this my station from
50.my sins, great and small,
51.my evil deeds, inward and outward,
52.my lapses, past and recent,

segments.arabic: تَوْبَةَ مَنْ لا يُحَدِّثُ نَفْسَهُ بِمَعْصِيَةٍ

translation.en: 53.with the repentance of one who does not tell himself that he might disobey

segments.arabic: وَلاَ يُضْمِرُ أَنْ يَعُودَ فِي خَطِيئَةٍ،

translation.en: 54.or secretly think that he might return to an offense.

segments.arabic: وَقَدْ قُلْتَ يَا إلهِي فِي مُحْكَمِ كِتابِكَ

translation.en: 55.You hast said, my God, in the firm text of Your Book,
56.that You 'acceptest repentance from Your servants,
57.pardonest evil deeds' (ref.42:25), and lovest the repenters (ref.2:222),

segments.arabic: فَاقْبَلْ تَوْبَتِي كَمَا وَعَدْتَ وَأعْفُ عَنْ سَيِّئاتِي كَمَا ضَمِنْتَ،

translation.en: 58.so accept my repentance as You hast promised, pardon my evil deeds as You hast guaranteed,
59.and make obligatory toward me Your love as You hast stipulated!

segments.arabic: وَلَـكَ يَـا رَبِّ شَـرْطِي أَلاّ أَعُودَ فِي مَكْرُوهِكَ،

translation.en: 60.To You, my Lord, belongs my stipulation that I will not return to what is disliked by You,
61.my guarantee that I will not go back to what You blamest,
62.and my covenant that I will stay away from acts of disobedience to You.

segments.arabic: أللَّهُمَّ إنَّكَ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا عَمِلْتُ فَاغْفِرْ لِي مَا عَلِمْتَ،

translation.en: 63.O God, You knowest better what I have done, so forgive me what You knowest
64.and turn me through Your power to what You lovest!
65.O God, counted against me are claims that stay in my memory
66.and claims that I have forgotten,
67.while all of them remain in Your eye that does not sleep
68.and Your knowledge that does not forget!

segments.arabic: فَعَوِّضْ مِنْهَا أَهْلَهَا وَاحْطُطْ عَنّي وِزْرَهَا،

translation.en: 69.So compensate their owners, lighten their load upon me,

segments.arabic: وَخَفِّفْ عَنِّي ثِقْلَهَا، وَاعْصِمْنِي مِنْ أَنْ اُقَارِفَ مِثْلَهَا.

translation.en: 70.lift up their weight from me, and preserve me from approaching their like!

segments.arabic: أللَّهُمَّ وَإنَّهُ لاَ وَفَاءَ لِي بِالتَّوْبَةِ إلاَّ بِعِصْمَتِكَ،

translation.en: 71.O God, but I can not be faithful to my repentance without Your preservation,

segments.arabic: وَلا اسْتِمْسَاكَ بِي عَنِ الْخَطَايَا إلاَّ عَنْ قُوَّتِكَ،

translation.en: 72.nor can I refrain from offenses without Your strength.

segments.arabic: فَقَوِّنِي بِقُوَّة كَافِيَة، وَتَوَلَّنِي بِعِصْمَة مَانِعَة.

translation.en: 73.So strengthen me with a sufficient strength and attend to me with a defending preservation!
74.O God, if any servant repents to You,

segments.arabic: وَهُوَ فِي عِلْمِ الْغَيْبِ عِنْدَكَ فَاسِخٌ لِتَوْبَتِهِ

translation.en: 75.while in Your knowledge of the Unseen he will break his repentance
76.and return to his sin and offense,

segments.arabic: فَإنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ أنْ أَكُوْنَ كَذلِكَ،

translation.en: 77.I seek refuge in You lest I be like that!

segments.arabic: فَاجْعَلْ تَوْبَتِي هَذِهِ تَوْبَةً لا أَحْتَاجُ بَعْدَهَا إلَى تَوْبَة،

translation.en: 78.So make this my repentance a repentance after which I will need no repentance

segments.arabic: تَوْبَةً مُوجِبَةً لِمَحْوِ مَا سَلَفَ، وَالسَّلاَمَةِ فِيمَـا بَقِيَ.

translation.en: 79.and a repentance which will obligate the erasing of what has gone by and safety in what remains!

segments.arabic: أللَّهُمَّ إنِّي أَعْتَـذِرُ إلَيْـكَ مِنْ جَهْلِي،

translation.en: 80.O God, I ask pardon from You for my ignorance,
81.and I ask You to disregard my evil acts!
82.So join me to the shelter of Your mercy through graciousness
83.and cover me with the covering of Your well-being through bounteousness!
84.O God, I repent to You
85.from everything opposed to Your will
86.or far from Your love -
87.the thoughts of my heart,
88.the glances of my eye,
89.the tales of my tongue -

segments.arabic: تَوْبَةً تَسْلَمُ بِهَا كُلُّ جَارِحَة عَلَى حِيَالِهَا مِنْ تَبِعَاتِكَ،

translation.en: 90.with a repentance through which each bodily part will by itself stay safe from ill consequences with You

segments.arabic: وَتَأْمَنُ مِمَّا يَخَافُ الْمُعْتَدُونَ مِنْ أَلِيْمِ سَطَوَاتِكَ.

translation.en: 91.and remain secure from Your painful penalties feared by transgressors!
92.O God, so have mercy on my being alone before You,
93.the pounding of my heart in dread of You,
94.the trembling of my limbs in awe of You!

segments.arabic: فَقَدْ أَقَامَتْنِي يَا رَبِّ ذُنُوبِي مَقَامَ الْخِزْيِ بِفِنَائِكَ،

translation.en: 95.My sins, my God, have stood me in the station of degradation in Your courtyard.

segments.arabic: فَإنْ سَكَتُّ لَمْ يَنْطِقْ عَنِّي أَحَدٌ،

translation.en: 96.If I remain silent, none will speak for me;
97.if I seek an intercessor, I am not worthy for intercession.
98.O God, bless Muhammad and his Household,
99.make Your generosity intercede for my offenses,
100.follow up my evil deeds with Your pardon,
101.repay me not with the punishment that is my proper repayment,
102.spread over me Your graciousness, wrap me in Your covering,

segments.arabic: وَافْعَلْ بِي فِعْلَ عَزِيز تَضَرَّع إلَيْهِ عَبْدٌ ذَلِيلٌ فَرَحِمَهُ،

translation.en: 103.and do with me what is done by a mighty man, when a lowly slave pleads to him and he shows him mercy,

segments.arabic: أَوْ غَنِيٍّ تَعَرَّضَ لَهُ عَبْدٌ فَقِيرٌ فَنَعَشَهُ.

translation.en: 104.or a rich man, when a poor slave submits himself and he raises him to wealth!

segments.arabic: أللَّهُمَّ لاَ خَفِيرَ لِي مِنْكَ فَلْيَخْفُرْنِيْ عِزُّكَ،

translation.en: 105.O God, I have no protector against You, so let Your might be my protector!

segments.arabic: وَلا شَفِيعَ لِيْ إلَيْكَ فَلْيَشْفَعْ لِي فَضْلُكَ،

translation.en: 106.I have no intercessor with You, so let Your bounty be my intercessor!
107.My offenses have set me quaking, so let Your pardon give me security!

segments.arabic: فَمَا كُلُّ مَا نَطَقْتُ بِهِ عَنْ جَهْل مِنِّي بِسُوْءِ أَثَرِي،

translation.en: 108.Not all that I have said rises up from my ignorance of my evil footsteps

segments.arabic: وَلاَ نِسيَان لِمَا سَبَقَ مِنْ ذَمِيمِ فِعْلِي،

translation.en: 109.or forgetfulness of my blameworthy acts in the past,
110.but in order that Your heaven and those within it

segments.arabic: وَأَرْضُكَ وَمَنْ عَلَيْهَا مَا أَظْهَرْتُ لَكَ مِنَ النَّدَمِ،

translation.en: 111.and Your earth and those upon it may hear the remorse which I have professed to You
112.and the repentance through which I have sought asylum with You.

segments.arabic: فَلَعَـلَّ بَعْضَهُمْ بِرَحْمَتِكَ يَرْحَمُنِي لِسُوءِ مَوْقِفِي،

translation.en: 113.Then perhaps one of them, through Your mercy, may show mercy upon my evil situation

segments.arabic: أَوْ تُدْرِكُهُ الرِّقَّةُ عَلَىَّ لِسُوءِ حَالِي

translation.en: 114.or be seized by tenderness for my evil state.

segments.arabic: فَيَنَالَنِي مِنْهُ بِدَعْوَةٍ أَسْمَعُ لَدَيْكَ مِنْ دُعَائِي،

translation.en: 115.There may come from him for my sake a supplication to which You givest ear more than to my supplication

segments.arabic: أَوْ شَفَاعَـةٍ أَوْكَدُ عِنْدَكَ مِنْ شَفَاعَتِي

translation.en: 116.or an intercession surer with You than my intercession

segments.arabic: تَكُونُ بِهَا نَجَاتِي مِنْ غَضَبِكَ وَفَوْزَتِي بِرضَاكَ.

translation.en: 117.through which I may be delivered from Your wrath and attain to Your good pleasure!

segments.arabic: أللَّهُمَّ إنْ يَكُنِ النَّدَمُ تَوْبَةً إلَيْكَ فَأَنَا أَنْدَمُ اْلنَّادِمِينَ،

translation.en: 118.O God, if remorse is a repentance toward You, then I am the most remorseful of the remorseful!

segments.arabic: وَإنْ يَكُنِ التَّرْكُ لِمَعْصِيَتِكَ إنَابَةً فَأَنَا أَوَّلُ الْمُنِيبينَ،

translation.en: 119.If refraining from disobedience is a turning back to You, then I am the first of those who turn back!

segments.arabic: وَإنْ يَكُنِ الاسْتِغْفَارُ حِطَّةً لِلذُّنُوبِ فَإنَي لَكَ مِنَ الْمُسْتَغْفِرِينَ.

translation.en: 120.If praying for forgiveness alleviates sins, surely I am one of those who pray for Your forgiveness!

segments.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ فَكَمَا أَمَرْتَ بِالتَّوْبَةِ وَضَمِنْتَ الْقَبُولَ

translation.en: 121.O God, as You hast commanded repentance and guaranteed acceptance,
122.as You hast urged supplication, and promised to respond,

segments.arabic: فَصَلِّ عَلَى مُحَمَّدِ وَآلِهِ وَاقْبَلْ تَوْبَتِي

translation.en: 123.so also bless Muhammad and his Household, accept my repentance,

segments.arabic: وَلاَ تَرْجِعْني مَرجَعَ الخَيبَةِ منْ رَحْمَتِك

translation.en: 124.and return me not to the returning place of disappointment in Your mercy!
125.Surely You art "Ever-turning" (2:128) toward the sinners,
126."All-compassionate" (2:128) toward the offenders who turn back!

segments.arabic: أللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَى مُحَمَّد وَآلِهِ كَمَا هَدَيْتَنَا بِهِ

translation.en: 127.O God, bless Muhammad and his Household just as You hast guided us by him!

segments.arabic: وَصَلِّ عَلَى مُحَمَّد وَآلِهِ كَمَا اسْتَنْقَذْتَنَا بِهِ،

translation.en: 128.Bless Muhammad and his Household just as You hast rescued us through him!
129.Bless Muhammad and his Household,

segments.arabic: صَلاَةً تَشْفَعُ لَنَا يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ وَيَوْمَ الْفَاقَةِ إلَيْكَ،

translation.en: 130.with a blessing that will intercede for us on the Day of Resurrection, the day of neediness toward You!

segments.arabic: إنَّكَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْء قَدِيرٌ وَهُوَ عَلَيْكَ يَسِيرٌ .

translation.en: 131."Thou art powerful over everything" (3:26), and that is easy for You!
1.O God, I ask pardon from You for

segments.arabic: مِنْ مَـظْلُوم ظُلِمَ بِحَضْرَتِي فَلَمْ أَنْصُرْهُ،

translation.en: 2.the person wronged in my presence whom I did not help,

segments.arabic: وَمِنْ مَعْرُوف أُسْدِيَ إلَيَّ فَلَمْ أَشْكُرْهُ،

translation.en: 3.the favour conferred upon me for which I returned no thanks,

segments.arabic: وَمِنْ مُسِيء أعْتَذَرَ إلَيَّ فَلَمْ أَعْذِرْهُ،

translation.en: 4.the evildoer who asked pardon from me and whom I did not pardon,

segments.arabic: وَمِنْ ذِيْ فَاقَة سَأَلَنِي فَلَمْ اُوثِرْهُ،

translation.en: 5.the needy person who asked from me and whom I preferred not over myself,

segments.arabic: وَمِنْ حَقِّ ذي حَقٍّ لَزِمَنِي لِمُؤْمِن فَلَمْ أوَفِّـرْهُ،

translation.en: 6.the right of a believer who possesses a right incumbent upon me which I did not fulfil,

segments.arabic: وَمِنْ عَيْبِ مُؤْمِن ظَهَر لِي فَلَمْ أَسْتُرْهُ،

translation.en: 7.the fault of a believer which became evident to me and which I did not conceal,

segments.arabic: وَمِنْ كُلِّ إثْم عَرَضَ لِيْ فَلَمْ أَهْجُرْهُ.

translation.en: 8.and every sin which presented itself to me and which I failed to avoid.
9.I ask pardon, my God, for all of these
10.and their likes, with an asking of pardon in remorse

segments.arabic: يَكُونُ وَاعِظاً لِمَا بَيْنَ يَدَيَّ مِنْ أَشْبَاهِهِنَّ.

translation.en: 11.which may act as an admonisher against similar things ahead of me.
12.So bless Muhammad and his Household

segments.arabic: وَاجْعَلْ نَدَامَتِي عَلَى مَا وَقَعْتُ فِيهِ مِنَ الـزَّلاّتِ

translation.en: 13.and make my remorse for the slips into which I have fallen

segments.arabic: وَعَزْمِي عَلَى تَـرْكِ مَا يَعْـرِضُ لِيْ مِنَ السَّيِّئـاتِ

translation.en: 14.and my determination to refrain from the evil deeds which present themselves to me
15.a repentance which will make Your love for me obligatory,
16.O lover of those who repent!
1.O God, bless Muhammad and his Household,
2.break my passion for every unlawful thing,
3.take away my craving for any sin,

segments.arabic: وَامْنَعْنِي عَنْ أَذَى كُـلِّ مُؤْمِن وَمُؤْمِنَـة وَمُسْلِم وَمُسْلِمَة،

translation.en: 4.and bar me from harming any believer, male or female, and any Muslim, male or female!

segments.arabic: أللَّهُمَّ وَأَيُّمَا عَبْد نالَ مِنِّي مَا حَظَرْتَ عَلَيْهِ،

translation.en: 5.O God, if any of Your servants should harm me in what You hast forbidden
6.or violate me in what You hast interdicted,
7.and if he should pass into death with my complaint
8.or I come to have a complaint against him while he is alive,

segments.arabic: فَاغْفِرْ لَهُ مَا أَلَمَّ بِهِ مِنِّي،

translation.en: 9.forgive him what he did to me

segments.arabic: وَاعْفُ لَهُ عَمَّا أَدْبَرَ بِهِ عَنِّي،

translation.en: 10.and pardon him that through which he turned his back on me!

segments.arabic: وَلاَ تَقِفْـهُ عَلَى مَا ارْتَكَبَ فِيَّ،

translation.en: 11.Inquire not from him about what he committed toward me
12.and expose him not through what he earned by me!

segments.arabic: وَاجْعَلْ مَا سَمَحْتُ بِهِ مِنَ الْعَفْـوِ عَنْهُمْ

translation.en: 13.Make my open-handedness in pardoning such servants
14.and my contribution in charity toward them
15.the purest charity of the charitable
16.and the highest gift of those seeking nearness to You!
17.Recompense me for my pardoning them with Your pardon
18.and for my supplicating for them with Your mercy

segments.arabic: حَتَّى يَسْعَدَ كُلُّ وَاحِد مِنَّا بِفَضْلِكَ

translation.en: 19.so that each one of us may gain felicity through Your bounty
20.and each may attain deliverance through Your kindness!
21.O God, if there is a servant from among Your servants

segments.arabic: أدرَكَهُ مِنِّي دَرَكٌ أَوْ مَسَّهُ مِنْ نَاحِيَتِي أَذَىً،

translation.en: 22.whom an ill visits on my account, a harm touches from my direction,

segments.arabic: أَوْ لَحِقَـهُ بِي أَوْ بِسَبَبي ظُلْمٌ

translation.en: 23.or a wrong overtakes through me or because of me,
24.and should I fail to take care of his right
25.or go before him [in death] with his complaint,
26.bless Muhammad and his Household,

segments.arabic: وَأَرْضِهِ عَنِّي مِنْ وُجْدِكَ، وَأَوْفِهِ حَقَّهُ مِنْ عِنْدِكَ،

translation.en: 27.satisfy him toward me through Your wealth, and give him his full right from Yourself!

segments.arabic: ثُمَّ قِنيْ مَا يُوجِبُ لَهُ حُكْمُكَ،

translation.en: 28.Then protect me from what Your decision mandates
29.and save me from what Your justice decides,
30.for my strength cannot bear Your vengeance
31.and my power cannot stand up to Your displeasure!
32.If You recompensest me with the right, You wilt destroy me,
33.and if You dost not shield me in Your mercy, You wilt lay me waste.

segments.arabic: أللَّهُمَّ إنِّي أَسْتَوْهِبُكَ يَا إلهِي مَا لا يَنْقُصُكَ بَذْلُهُ،

translation.en: 34.O God, I ask You to grant, my God, that whose giving will not decrease You,
35.and I ask You to carry that whose carrying will not weigh You down:

segments.arabic: أَسْتَوْهِبُكَ يَا إلهِي نَفْسِيَ الَّتِيْ لَمْ تَخْلُقْهَا لِتَمْتَنِعَ بِهَا مِنْ سُوء،

translation.en: 36.My God, I ask You to give my soul, which You didst not create to keep Yourself from evil
37.nor to find the way to profit.

segments.arabic: وَلكِنْ أَنْشَأتَهَا إثْبَاتاً لِقُدْرَتِكَ عَلَى مِثْلِهَا،

translation.en: 38.No, You brought it forth to demonstrate Your power over its like
39.and to provide an argument against its similar.

segments.arabic: وَأَسْتَحْمِلُكَ مِنْ ذُنُوبِي مَا قَدْ بَهَظَنِي حَمْلُهُ،

translation.en: 40.I ask You to carry those of my sins whose carrying weighs me down

segments.arabic: وَأَسْتَعِينُ بِكَ عَلَى مَا قَدْ فَدَحَنِي ثِقْلُهُ.

translation.en: 41.and I seek help from You in that whose heaviness oppresses me.
42.So bless Muhammad and his Household,
43.give to me my soul in spite of its wrongdoing,
44.and appoint Your mercy to carry my burden!
45.How many evildoers Your mercy has overtaken!
46.How many wrongdoers Your pardon has embraced!

segments.arabic: فَصَلِّ عَلَى مُحَمَّد وَآلِهِ، وَاجْعَلْنِي أُسْوَةَ مَنْ قَدْ أَنْهَضْتَهُ

translation.en: 47.So bless Muhammad and his Household and make me the model of him whom You hast aroused
48.through Your forbearance from the deadly infirmities of the offenders
49.and saved through Your giving success from the tangled plights of the sinners,

segments.arabic: فَأَصْبَحَ طَلِيقَ عَفْوِكَ مِنْ إسَارِ سُخْطِكَ،

translation.en: 50.so that I may rise up freed by Your pardon from the bonds of Your displeasure
51.and released by Your benefaction from the ties of Your justice!

segments.arabic: إنَّكَ إنْ تَفْعَلْ ذَلِكَ يَا إلهِي تَفْعَلْهُ بِمَنْ

translation.en: 52.Surely if You dost that, my God, You wilt do it to one
53.who does not deny deserving Your punishment

segments.arabic: وَلاَ يُبَرِّئُ نَفْسَهُ مِنِ اسْتِيجَابِ نَقِمَتِكَ،

translation.en: 54.or acquit himself from merit for Your vengeance.

segments.arabic: تَفْعَلُ ذلِكَ يَا إلهِي بِمَنْ خَوْفُهُ مِنْكَ أَكْثَرُ مِنْ طَمَعِهِ فِيكَ،
